title: Simple Panel Bed (All Mattress Sizes)
summary: A panel bed with 1x8-clad headboard and footboard, capped with a 1x3 top rail — no pocket holes anywhere, glue and nails doing the clean-look work instead. Queen dimensions here.
description:
A 'panel bed' earns its name from the headboard: horizontal 1x8s stacked edge to edge over 2x4 legs, glued along every seam, capped with a 1x3 top rail that hides all the end grain. This design gets that clean furniture look with zero pocket holes — the fastener strategy is glue plus brads where looks matter (easy to fill, easy to hide), and 3" screws where structure matters.

The layout trick in step 1 does the aligning: a pencil line ripped down each headboard leg, 2-1/4" in from the outside edge, gives every panel board the same registration — no measuring per board, no creep. And the glue between the 1x8s' edges is not optional: it's what turns five separate boards into one stiff panel that won't develop gaps or rattle.

This is the queen version; other sizes change lengths only. The bed frame inside is the standard center-rail system — cleats on the side rails, a legged 2x4 spine down the middle, and 2x4 slats every 4" (generous, and generous is right: 2x4 slats at that spacing will outlive the mattress). The spacer-block tip in step 5 is the kind of small thing that makes solo assembly possible — cut it, use it, thank it.

tools.note: Nails hide easier than screws on the visible panels — the plan prefers them wherever looks matter.
The 2-1/4" registration line down each leg is the whole alignment system.

materials.name: 1x8, 10 ft
materials.note: Panel boards — can be cut in half at the store for transport.

materials.name: 2x4, 8 ft
materials.note: Legs, cleats, center frame — six of these become the bed slats.

materials.name: Brad nails, 1-1/4", 18 ga
materials.note: Or 2" self-tapping screws where the nailer can't reach.

materials.name: Self-tapping wood screws, #10 x 3", star drive
Wood glue
materials.note: Every panel seam gets a bead — it's what makes five boards act like one panel.

cutList.note: Three on the headboard, one on the footboard (queen).
Cut to fit your assembled bed.
1x4s or 1x3s substitute if cheaper or on hand.

steps.title: Panel the headboard off a pencil line
steps.body:
Lay out the two headboard legs and strike a pencil line down each, 2-1/4" in from the outside edge — every panel board's end registers to it.

Dry-fit all the panel boards first. Then glue and fasten the TOP 1x8 to the legs, and work downward, running a bead of glue along each board-to-board edge as you go. That seam glue is the panel's real strength.

steps.title: Cap the headboard
steps.body: Glue the headboard's whole top edge, set the 1x3 cap with even overhangs at the ends and flush at the back, and fasten — brads if you have the nailer (they vanish under filler), screws if you don't.

steps.title: Build the footboard
steps.body: Same construction at ankle height: one panel board across the short legs, capped with the other 1x3.

steps.title: Build the side rails
steps.body: Glue each 2x4 cleat and lay a 1x8 side rail on top of it; fasten from the outside — nails preferred, again for the hiding. The cleat is the structure; the 1x8 is the face.

steps.title: Join rails to headboard and footboard
steps.body: Screw the side rails to the legs with the 3" screws. The plan's sanity-saver: cut a spacer block from 2x4 scrap to hold the rail at its height while you drive — solo assembly goes from juggling to routine.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, #10 x 3", star drive

steps.title: Add the headboard frame rail
steps.body: Measure the actual width between the cleats at the headboard (about 57" on a queen, but measure yours), cut the 2x4 to fit, and screw it to the headboard. It's the fourth side of the slat-support rectangle.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, #10 x 3", star drive

steps.title: Build the center spine
steps.body: Screw the two 12-1/2" legs to the 78-1/2" center support with 3" screws — a T-section beam that carries the middle of every slat.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, #10 x 3", star drive

steps.title: Install the spine
steps.body: Center the spine down the bed and screw it to the headboard frame rail and footboard with 3" screws. Its top must land level with the side cleats — the slats sit on all three.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, #10 x 3", star drive

steps.title: Slat it
steps.body:
Lay the eighteen 2x4 slats across cleats and spine, about 4" apart, and screw down with 3" screws. 2x4 slats at 4" spacing is deliberate overkill — this platform will never bow, squeak, or care what mattress you put on it.

Fill the brad holes, sand, finish, and slide the mattress in.
materials: Self-tapping wood screws, #10 x 3", star drive

images.alt: Wood panel bed with horizontal plank headboard and low footboard
